text: The surgeon performs robotic excision of the paraganglioma mass in the right lateral position, using four 8mm ports and one 12mm assistant port. After mobilizing the colon and exposing the renal hilum, the mass is visible between the renal vessels. The lower part of the mass is tackled, clearing the aortic border, with potential clipping of renal vessels if necessary for removal of the mass along with the kidney.
The surgeon carefully dissects the mass, which is visibly adherent to both the renal vein and artery, highlighting the complexity of separating these structures during the adrenalectomy procedure.

The surgeon clamps the renal vessels to facilitate safe dissection of the mass from the vessels. During the dissection, the renal artery is visibly injured. Further dissection leads to the decision to excise a segment of the renal vein along with the mass.

Following the excision of the tumor, the surgeon performs an end-to-end anastomosis of the renal vein using Gore-Tex CV-VI suture in a continuous manner. The rent in the renal artery is repaired with Gore-Tex CV-Six suture.
